rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrochemical radical thiocyanation/cyclization of N-allyl pyrazoles with NaSCN to afford SCN-functionalized pyrazolo-fused heterocycles via radical pathway
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of SCN- (assisted by photoexcited TPPT acting as single-electron oxidant) generates thiocyanate radical (and/or (SCN)2); the radical adds to the alkene, triggering radical cyclization onto the arene followed by rearomatization

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered radical from SCN addition to alkene
cyclohexadienyl radical after intramolecular arene attack

electron_flow: Photoexcited TPPT* oxidizes SCN- to SCN• (TPPT is regenerated at the anode); SCN• adds to the terminal alkene of 1a to give a tertiary C-radical that cyclizes onto the pendant arene, and rearomatization (H loss/oxidation) delivers product 3a.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 3a dropped to 0% (vs 70% without quencher)

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield <1%; (2-thiocyanatoethene-1,1-diyl)dibenzene 6 isolated in 75%, confirming SCN• generation

probe_reagents.name: (SCN)2 (NCS-SCN, thiocyanogen)
probe_reagents.observation: Used in place of NaSCN gave 3a in 64% NMR yield, indicating (SCN)2 can serve as a competent but less reactive intermediate; alternative pathways not via (SCN)2 may also operate

notes: On-off experiments show both light AND current are required for sustained reactivity (light-off with continuous electrolysis stalls; electricity-off with continuous irradiation also stalls), consistent with a photoelectrochemical cycle where TPPT must be both photoexcited and electrochemically regenerated. (SCN)2 is shown to be a competent intermediate but is less reactive than NaSCN, so an alternative (non-(SCN)2) pathway involving direct anodic/photoredox oxidation of SCN- to SCN• is likely operative in parallel.
